message:
perf(http): replace windows()-based CRLF scanning with dedicated byte-level search functions

The HTTP/1.1 codec used buf.windows(2).position(|w| w == b"\r\n") and
buf.windows(4).position(|w| w == b"\r\n\r\n") for locating line terminators
and header block boundaries. While correct, these create an iterator of
overlapping slices and compare each window via PartialEq, which the compiler
does not always optimize to a simple byte scan.

Introduces two dedicated functions:
- find_headers_end(): scans for \r\n\r\n with direct byte comparisons,
  replacing the windows(4) pattern in header boundary detection
- find_crlf(buf, start): scans for \r\n from a given offset with direct
  byte comparisons, replacing the windows(2) pattern in 5 call sites:
  split_line_crlf, request-line length check, request-line extraction,
  and header line iteration in decode_head()

The decode_head() header parsing loop is also simplified: find_crlf() now
returns the absolute index of the \r byte rather than a relative offset,
eliminating the cursor arithmetic (line_start/line_end variables) that
previously translated relative positions to absolute slice ranges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
